text.text:
Had the Cucumbers with XO sauce, beef noodle soup, Miyazaki wagyu fried rice, xiao long bao, pork wontons in chili sauce.

All were pretty good. Not the best I've had but it was still pretty solid.

My favorites were the beef noodle soup and the pork wontons. The fried rice was pretty good too. The rice were separately well! The cucumbers needed some more acidity. The XLB was porky and yummy but I wish they had a sampler of each of their XLB flavors but I got spoiled by Paradise Dynasty.

The service was great and that you can order through a tablet. The menu was designed well and I like that the menu isn't as extensive so you're limited to what you can eat. I hate long menus because there's too many to look at and try.

The atmosphere was great. Lots of great design inside and they were playing young JB, Selena music when i went. I won't drive out to come back but if in the area and someone wants to go, I'll come with them.

text.text:
Second time here and tried different dishes, still a gem! We ordered different dishes to try the carbs and proteins and the meal was satisfying.
Must try: kurobota pork&crab meat soup dumplings, wagyu beef chinese burger, dried scallops&egg white fried rice.
If want something fancier, go with the beef short rib with lime sauce, very tender and well-seasoned meat.
Big surprise: the beverages! Will definitely come back just for the milk tea and slushy! Tried the cucumber glutinous rice mochi icy slush and the oolong milk tea, both very tasty!!
Seating was fast, even lines were long. Service is excellent too, food serving is very fast, 5 mins after we ordered the first batch we got our first dish. Servers always come around and gave us what we needed.
Would love to come back for third, fourth time!

text.text:
The kinda asian place you take your non-asian friends to, if you know what I mean.

The price to food permance ratio could be a bit better given it's competition around Rosemead area. However you are paying for the atmosphere, the service, the new interiors, the easily readable menu.

Taste of everything was on a good level. Nothing wrong there.

text.text:
Food Type: Shanghai Style Xiao Long Bao
Portions: Small
Price: $15-$25 ish
Service: Good
Atmosphere: Basis Design simple restaurant theme. Open area for making xiaolongbao. Seems to be the theme for this kind of cuisines

The xiaolongbao is good with tasty soup inside. Taste just right, not so salty, but flavorful. Be careful it's hot. The mini rib is different than others, it's cut into large size, I like the size, however, it's a bit sweet and maybe a tough too much on the vinegar. Maybe just me. Cauliflower is good, good wok qi, but it's a bit salty and I agreed with my friend as well.  The other 2 dish, it's ok, as I am not big in spicy food and that noodle is ok.
One disappointment, the menu is not large. They are missing the fried dumplings which I like and was expecting to have some. Not on the menu. Also, missing the green onion pancake. Love those, but can't get it there

Overall: I think it's ok, nothing special or stands out for me. Don't mind eating there again, but i wouldn't choose this place over others if I felt like xiaolongbao again, but I do want my pan fried dumplings.

text.text:
In order to stand out in the San Gabriel Valley a Restaurant must either be exceptional or unique, and under the direction of David Shao and Chef Peter Pang Good Alley achieves both while also offering friendly service inside a modern dining room.

Tucked away in a Valley Boulevard strip mall, the open kitchen abutting roughly sixty seats where iPad ordering simplifies staffing, guests are greeted with a smile before perusing a lengthy list of Noodles, Dumplings, Meat and Rice Dishes.

Proudly making everything by hand, their limited number of Guan Tang Bao prone to early weekend sell-outs, it is after a bit of scrolling that in-the-know diners will note an Ingredient-focused approach including Kurobata Pork for a richer Xiao Long Bao.

谷巷里 in Chinese, the owners of Jirong Roast Duck Restaurant opening Good Alley in September of 2024, additional plates well-suited for solo diners include a simple bowl of hand-pulled Noodles slicked in Scallion Oil with Vegetables or the faultless Beef Roll wrapped in Roti-like Pancakes plus one of several Slushies or Teas to drink.
